nodes.x: In thee, O Lord, do I put my trust: let me never be put to confusion.
Preserve me, O God: for in thee do I put my trust.
In thee, O Lord, do I put my trust; let me never be ashamed: deliver me in thy righteousness.
O Lord my God, in thee do I put my trust: save me from all them that persecute me, and deliver me:
That thy trust may be in the Lord, I have made known to thee this day, even to thee.
But I trusted in thee, O Lord: I said, Thou art my God.
Deliver me in thy righteousness, and cause me to escape: incline thine ear unto me, and save me.
Deliver me from bloodguiltiness, O God, thou God of my salvation: and my tongue shall sing aloud of thy righteousness.
Deliver me, O Lord, from mine enemies: I flee unto thee to hide me.
Incline your ear, and come unto me: hear, and your soul shall live; and I will make an everlasting covenant with you, even the sure mercies of David.
Bow down thine ear to me; deliver me speedily: be thou my strong rock, for an house of defence to save me.
I will declare thy righteousness, and thy works; for they shall not profit thee.
Be thou my strong habitation, whereunto I may continually resort: thou hast given commandment to save me; for thou art my rock and my fortress.
For thou art my rock and my fortress; therefore for thy name’s sake lead me, and guide me.
Therefore shall ye keep all the commandments which I command you this day, that ye may be strong, and go in and possess the land, whither ye go to possess it;
I say, sayest thou, (but they are but vain words) I have counsel and strength for war: now on whom dost thou trust, that thou rebellest against me?

But as for thee, stand thou here by me, and I will speak unto thee all the commandments, and the statutes, and the judgments, which thou shalt teach them, that they may do them in the land which I give them to possess it.
Deliver me, O my God, out of the hand of the wicked, out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.
And I will deliver thee out of the hand of the wicked, and I will redeem thee out of the hand of the terrible.
Deliver me, O Lord, from the evil man: preserve me from the violent man;
God hath delivered me to the ungodly, and turned me over into the hands of the wicked.
I have therefore delivered him into the hand of the mighty one of the heathen; he shall surely deal with him: I have driven him out for his wickedness.
O wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me from the body of this death?
For thou art my hope, O Lord God: thou art my trust from my youth.

¶ Rejoice, O young man, in thy youth; and let thy heart cheer thee in the days of thy youth, and walk in the ways of thine heart, and in the sight of thine eyes: but know thou, that for all these things God will bring thee into judgment.
O Lord, thou art my God; I will exalt thee, I will praise thy name; for thou hast done wonderful things; thy counsels of old are faithfulness and truth.

And now, O my son Helaman, behold, thou art in thy youth, and therefore, I beseech of thee that thou wilt hear my words and learn of me; for I do know that whosoever shall put their trust in God shall be supported in their trials, and their troubles, and their afflictions, and shall be lifted up at the last day.
By thee have I been holden up from the womb: thou art he that took me out of my mother’s bowels: my praise shall be continually of thee.
Nevertheless I am continually with thee: thou hast holden me by my right hand.
I was cast upon thee from the womb: thou art my God from my mother’s belly.
But thou art he that took me out of the womb: thou didst make me hope when I was upon my mother’s breasts.
¶ His own iniquities shall take the wicked himself, and he shall be holden with the cords of his sins.
Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy right hand hath holden me up, and thy gentleness hath made me great.
I am as a wonder unto many; but thou art my strong refuge.
Thou art the God that doest wonders: thou hast declared thy strength among the people.
Thou art my hiding place and my shield: I hope in thy word.
Be thou my strong habitation, whereunto I may continually resort: thou hast given commandment to save me; for thou art my rock and my fortress.
Behold, the Lord will carry thee away with a mighty captivity, and will surely cover thee.

Behold, thou hast driven me out this day from the face of the earth; and from thy face shall I be hid; and I shall be a fugitive and a vagabond in the earth; and it shall come to pass, that every one that findeth me shall slay me.
Let my mouth be filled with thy praise and with thy honour all the day.
O Lord, open thou my lips; and my mouth shall shew forth thy praise.
Let every thing that hath breath praise the Lord. Praise ye the Lord.
And my tongue shall speak of thy righteousness and of thy praise all the day long.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise the Lord, O my soul.
My mouth shall speak the praise of the Lord: and let all flesh bless his holy name for ever and ever.
Cast me not off in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength faileth.
O spare me, that I may recover strength, before I go hence, and be no more.
My strength is dried up like a potsherd; and my tongue cleaveth to my jaws; and thou hast brought me into the dust of death.
I say unto you, unless this be the case, they must be cast off; and this I know, because I was like to be cast off.

Be careful of your bodies; be prudent in laying out your energies,
for when you are old you will need the strength and power you are now
wasting. Preserve your lives. Until you know and practice this, you are
not thoroughly good soldiers nor wise stewards. 8:136-137. {300}
When men are cast down, then thou shalt say, There is lifting up; and he shall save the humble person.
For mine enemies speak against me; and they that lay wait for my soul take counsel together,
Consider mine enemies; for they are many; and they hate me with cruel hatred.
Mine enemies reproach me all the day; and they that are mad against me are sworn against me.
¶ But I say unto you which hear, Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you,
But I say unto you which hear. Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you.
Mine enemies would daily swallow me up: for they be many that fight against me, O thou most High.
Saying, God hath forsaken him: persecute and take him; for there is none to deliver him.
The righteous perisheth, and no man layeth it to heart: and merciful men are taken away, none considering that the righteous is taken away from the evil to come.
Behold, he taketh away, who can hinder him? who will say unto him, What doest thou?

It is a common practice for men who persecute the people of God, io console themselves with the idea that they do not persecute them on account of their religion, but merely render them a just reward for their extreme politics or daring blasphemies. Very fipe 1 But let us consider. Whjr
And Ammon said unto him: No one hath told me, save it be God; and he said unto me—Go and deliver thy brethren, for they are in prison in the land of Middoni.
O God, be not far from me: O my God, make haste for my help.
Make haste, O God, to deliver me; make haste to help me, O Lord.
But be not thou far from me, O Lord: O my strength, haste thee to help me.
Be pleased, O Lord, to deliver me: O Lord, make haste to help me.
Make haste to help me, O Lord my salvation.
Forsake me not, O Lord: O my God, be not far from me.
Let them be confounded and consumed that are adversaries to my soul; let them be covered with reproach and dishonour that seek my hurt.
Let them be confounded and put to shame that seek after my soul: let them be turned back and brought to confusion that devise my hurt.
Let them be ashamed and confounded together that seek after my soul to destroy it; let them be driven backward and put to shame that wish me evil.
Let them be ashamed and confounded that seek after my soul: let them be turned backward, and put to confusion, that desire my hurt.
Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that rejoice at mine hurt: let them be clothed with shame and dishonour that magnify themselves against me.
But I will hope continually, and will yet praise thee more and more.
I will praise thee, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.
And now, Lord, what wait I for? my hope is in thee.
I will praise thee: for thou hast heard me, and art become my salvation.
I will praise thee; for I am fearfully and wonderfully made: marvellous are thy works; and that my soul knoweth right well.
I will praise thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will wait on thy name; for it is good before thy saints.
My mouth shall shew forth thy righteousness and thy salvation all the day; for I know not the numbers thereof.
And now I say unto you that the time shall come that the salvation of the Lord shall be declared to every nation, kindred, tongue, and people.
My tongue shall speak of thy word: for all thy commandments are righteousness.
For you shall live by every word that proceedeth forth from the mouth of God.
I will go in the strength of the Lord God: I will make mention of thy righteousness, even of thine only.
Open to me the gates of righteousness: I will go into them, and I will praise the Lord:
I will praise the Lord according to his righteousness: and will sing praise to the name of the Lord most high.
Hear my prayer, O Lord, give ear to my supplications: in thy faithfulness answer me, and in thy righteousness.
I will meditate in thy precepts, and have respect unto thy ways.
O God, thou hast taught me from my youth: and hitherto have I declared thy wondrous works.
Make me to understand the way of thy precepts: so shall I talk of thy wondrous works.
For thou art great, and doest wondrous things: thou art God alone.
Hearken unto this, O Job: stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God.
Open thou mine eyes, that I may behold wondrous things out of thy law.
Unto thee, O God, do we give thanks, unto thee do we give thanks: for that thy name is near thy wondrous works declare.
Now also when I am old and grayheaded, O God, forsake me not; until I have shewed thy strength unto this generation, and thy power to every one that is to come.
I said, O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days: thy years are throughout all generations.
that you have reached this advanced age, and rejoice that you arc preserved unto us in all the power of your glorious manhood and of your high and holy calling.
Now unto the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only wise God, be honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.
And now, behold, the king walketh before you: and I am old and grayheaded; and, behold, my sons are with you: and I have walked before you from my childhood unto this day.
Thy righteousness also, O God, is very high, who hast done great things: O God, who is like unto thee!
The Lord is righteous in all his ways, and holy in all his works.
Thy righteousness is like the great mountains; thy judgments are a great deep: O Lord, thou preservest man and beast.
Thinkest thou this to be right, that thou saidst, My righteousness is more than God’s?
Thou, which hast shewed me great and sore troubles, shalt quicken me again, and shalt bring me up again from the depths of the earth.
And shalt return unto the Lord thy God, and shalt obey his voice according to all that I command thee this day, thou and thy children, with all thine heart, and with all thy soul;
For now I will stretch out my hand, that I may smite thee and thy people with pestilence; and thou shalt be cut off from the earth.
For then shalt thou have thy delight in the Almighty, and shalt lift up thy face unto God.
I will extol thee, O Lord; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.
Thou shalt ascend and come like a storm, thou shalt be like a cloud to cover the land, thou, and all thy bands, and many people with thee.
Thou shalt increase my greatness, and comfort me on every side.
Thou shalt be perfect with the Lord thy God.
Therefore thou shalt love the Lord thy God, and keep his charge, and his statutes, and his judgments, and his commandments, alway.
Thou shalt guide me with thy counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.
If thou lovest me thou shalt serve me and keep all my commandments.
I will run the way of thy commandments, when thou shalt enlarge my heart.
I will also praise thee with the psaltery, even thy truth, O my God: unto thee will I sing with the harp, O thou Holy One of Israel.
Then will I go unto the altar of God, unto God my exceeding joy: yea, upon the harp will I praise thee, O God my God.
I will sing a new song unto thee, O God: upon a psaltery and an instrument of ten strings will I sing praises unto thee.
I will praise thee with my whole heart: before the gods will I sing praise unto thee.
I will praise thee, O Lord, among the people: and I will sing praises unto thee among the nations.
Sing unto the Lord with thanksgiving; sing praise upon the harp unto our God:
My lips shall greatly rejoice when I sing unto thee; and my soul, which thou hast redeemed.
My soul shall be satisfied as with marrow and fatness; and my mouth shall praise thee with joyful lips:
Yea, my reins shall rejoice, when thy lips speak right things.
My lips shall utter praise, when thou hast taught me thy statutes.
And my soul shall be joyful in the Lord: it shall rejoice in his salvation.
Rejoice, O my heart, and cry unto the Lord, and say: O Lord, I will praise thee forever; yea, my soul will rejoice in thee, my God, and the rock of my salvation.
My tongue also shall talk of thy righteousness all the day long: for they are confounded, for they are brought unto shame, that seek my hurt.
I have sworn by myself, the word is gone out of my mouth in righteousness, and shall not return, That unto me every knee shall bow, every tongue shall swear.
Let all their wickedness come before thee; and do unto them, as thou hast done unto me for all my transgressions: for my sighs are many, and my heart is faint.
